data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Novartis, Albemarle and Zscaler among top picks
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) closed in the green on Friday, while Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) rose 1% for the week. Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Novartis upgraded on stock pullback, Rhapsido launch

Morgan Stanley upgraded Novartis (NVS) to Overweight from Equal-weight, citing a valuation pullback after the Swiss drugmaker missed on the bottom line in its Q3 results in late October as well as optimism over the upcoming launch of Rhapsido (remibrutinib) for chronic spontaneous urticaria (hives).

Analyst Thibault Boutherin expects modest 2026 sales growth of 2% followed by 5% from 2027-2029, adding he sees the company projecting a 5% CAGR from 2025-2030.

Boutherin wrote that he anticipates $5B in risk adjusted potential sales for Rhapsido assuming additional indications, adding that the launch in chronic spontaneous urticaria is likely to surpass the consensus estimate. The bank has upped its price target to CHF 110 from CHF 108.

Albemarle upgraded on lithium demand optimism

Baird upgraded Albemarle (ALB) to Neutral from Underperform with a $113 price target, raised from $81, as excitement around energy storage boosts the demand outlook.

Albemarle shares have gained roughly a third over the past month, which Baird's Ben Kallo believes is due largely to optimism regarding lithium end-market demand tied to energy storage, and lithium companies and battery storage companies have cited improving conditions as the demand for storage solutions grows.

Kallo acknowledged an element of uncertainty regarding the supply picture in China but said he has seen fewer announcements regarding capacity additions and is "cautiously optimistic" that near-term announcements will not be made, given the relatively low pricing environment.

Regeneron downgraded on valuation

Morgan Stanley downgraded Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) to Equal-weight from Overweight saying that the stock is fairly valued.

Analyst Terence Flynn noted that Eylea HD (aflibercept) recently gaining an additional indication for macular edema following retinal vein occlusion, as well as US FDA approval of a monthly dosing option for all indications, along with strong Dupixent (dupilumab) sales, have helped shares rebound.

The brokerage maintained its PT of $767 on the stock.

Flynn added he would like to see more clarity on Regeneron's pipeline diversification. In 2026, he projects revenues of $14.89B (consensus $14.92B) and EPS of $45.19 (consensus $42.99).

Wall Street lowers PT for DocuSign despite 'solid' results

DocuSign (DOCU) was in focus after couple of Wall Street firms cut PTs on the electronic signature company, despite reporting what some called “solid” third-quarter results and guidance.

Wedbush kept their Neutral rating on the stock, but lowered PT to $75 from $85, while Piper Sandler lowered PT to $75 from $90.

“DOCU reported its FY3Q26 results featuring beats across the board as the company saw strong demand for IAM and eSignature, but the performance will be overshadowed by the relatively conservative outlook,” Wedbush said.

BofA moved its rating to Underperform from no rating on PagerDuty (PD), as it sees “limited catalysts” ahead. “Facing demand headwinds, a pricing model change, and an impending CFO transition, we think it could take multiple quarters for stabilization in key growth metrics, which could constrain investor sentiment and drive relative underperformance versus infrastructure software peers,” analyst Koji Ikeda said.

Humana (HUM) has been upgraded to Buy at Jefferies, driven by the company’s Stars diversification strategy.

Wells Fargo upgraded the video game software company Unity Software (U) to Overweight from Equal-Weight, citing favorable industry backdrop in 2026.

Zscaler (ZS) was downgraded by Bernstein to Market Perform from Outperform but kept his $264 price target, citing a “fair” valuation and a “challenged” narrative. Analyst Peter Weed said he is concerned the firm's growth is likely to slow down markedly over time.

data.title: Meta said to delay release of new mixed reality glasses named 'Phoenix' to 2027
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) has delayed the release of its new mixed reality glasses, code-named “Phoenix,” to the first half of 2027, Business Insider reported on Friday, citing an internal memo from an executive.

The memo was from Maher Saba, VP of Foundation and Head of Engineering at Meta (META), Business Insider said. The initial release of the product was planned in H2 2026.  

Business Insider said moving the release date back would give META "a lot more breathing room to get the details right," quoting from a separate memo it saw by Metaverse executives Gabriel Aul and Ryan Cairns.

Meta (META) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment. 

The Business Insider report comes two days after Bloomberg News said META was considering budget cuts of up to 30% in its Metaverse division, a segment that has seen $73B in cumulative losses since its inception.

data.title: DigitalBridge, Sezzle jump, American Bitcoin, WRB slide: week's financials wrap
data.description:
This week's biggest financials gainers were led by an alternative asset manager in takeover talks, while the biggest loser was a Trump family-tied bitcoin (BTC-USD) accumulation platform company.

DigitalBridge Group (DBRG) rose the most of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B) in the week ended Dec. 5, 2025, surging 45% after a report that Softbank was in talks to buy the firm, in a potential deal that could fetch $25-$35 per share;

Tech-enabled payments company Sezzle (SEZL) climbed 11%;

Financial advisory and asset management firm Lazard (LAZ) gained 8.4%;

Blue Owl Capital (OWL), an alternative asset manager, advanced 8.3%; and

U.K.-based financial services platform provider Marex Group (MEX) gapped up 7.7%.

Meanwhile, American Bitcoin (ABTC) led the losers by a wide margin, with a 47% loss;

W. R. Berkley (WR) retreated 14%;

Baldwin Insurance Group (BWIN), which during the week agreed to merge with CAC Group in a $1.03B deal, gapped down 12%;

Brazilian lender Banco Bradesco (BBD) slid 11%; and

XP (XP), a Brazil-based provider of financial products and services, rounded out the five biggest decliners with a 9.3% drop.

data.title: Elon Musk says 'EU should be abolished' days after Europe slaps $140M fine on X
data.description:
Elon Musk on Saturday said the European Union ((EU)) should be abolished two days after the body's governing agency slapped a €120M ($139.7M) fine on the billionaire businessman's X company for breaching transparency rules. 

The European Commission on Thursday said X had breached its transparency obligations under the Digital Services Act ((DSA)), including deceiving users with the use of its 'blue checkmark,' the lack of transparency of its advertising repository, and failing to provide access to public data for researchers.

"The EU should be abolished and sovereignty returned to individual countries, so that governments can better represent their people," Musk said on Saturday on his X account.  

"How long before the EU is gone? AbolishTheEU," he added later in a separate post. 

The commission's move against X comes just over three months after it slapped a whopping €2.95B ($3.43B) fine on Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google for breaching EU antitrust rules. 

The EU has continued to ramp up its crackdown against major U.S. technology companies, despite complaints from President Donald Trump about the investigations. Europe is known to have some of the most stringent and comprehensive technology regulations in the world.

Musk's X competes with other social media platforms such as Meta's (META) Facebook, Instagram, and Threads; content-focused sites such as ByteDance's (BDNCE) TikTok and Reddit (RDDT); and Microsoft's (MSFT) professional networking service LinkedIn.

Here are some exchange-traded funds tied to the technology sector: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT).

data.title: U.S. gov't investment in xLight may be long-term negative for ASML: Jefferies
data.description:
The Trump Administration's announcement that it will invest $150M into xLight could have broader implications for the global semiconductor industry, and in particular, ASML (ASML), investment firm Jefferies said.

“While too early to say if the technology will be successful, the backing of the U.S. government, access to U.S. National Lab technologies and Albany Nanotech's support increases the odds,” Jefferies analyst Janardan Menon wrote in a note to clients. “If successful, it could reduce demand for ASML’s [extreme ultraviolet lithography] scanners in the longer term due to its significant increase in productivity and yield of ASML scanners.”

The xLight investment is coming from the U.S. Commerce Dept. and is notable for a multitude of reasons: the company is developing free-electron laser technology seen as critical to producing faster, more advanced semiconductor chips; it counts former Intel (INTC) Pat Gelsinger as its executive chairman; and it is the first investment by the Trump Administration from the CHIPS Research and Development Office, which was created as part of the 2022 CHIPs Act. 

ASML's extreme ultraviolet lithography have a light source that generates light at 13.5 nanometers wavelength and uses laser produced plasma. Conversely, xLight's approach involves free electron lasers, which it claims are up to four times more powerful than ASML's method. The startup also said that it has programmable light characteristics, which could shorten the wavelengths beyond 13.5 nanometers.

If xLight's technology proves successful and is widely adopted, it could allow semiconductor foundries to have higher throughput and yield than what is current with ASML's machines. It would also mean fewer of ASML's extreme ultraviolet lithography scanners are needed, Menon suggested. He has a Hold rating and $400 price target on ASML.

“It could also shrink nodes at a lower cost by moving to smaller wavelengths, compared to ASML’s roadmap of staying at 13.5nm wavelength, but moving to high NA and hyper NA where system costs escalate sharply,” Menon added.

How it would work

If xLight is successful, its method may involve building a large (100 meter by 50 meter) facility that contains a particle accelerator and free electron lasers light source, Menon said. This potential facility could have enough light for 20 of ASML's extreme ultraviolet lithography systems. It's also fully backwards compatible, xLight has said previously. If the prototype works, both xLight and the U.S. government could retrofit ASML's EUV machines in the U.S. with the light source.

Of course, ASML is not likely to sit idly by, Menon conceded, with the high likelihood that it will “support development” of xLight. 

“Even though the success of the technology could result in a lower number of system sales due to its higher productivity, as well as potentially a lower ASP per system sold to U.S. fabs due to the lack of a light source, we would expect the company to be cooperative due to likely pressure from the US government,” Menon wrote. “If the technology is successful, we do not expect the US government to allow it to be widely used outside the U.S., as the key objective is to enable U.S. leadership in semiconductor manufacturing technology and [companies].”

Of course, the eventual deployment of xLight is still a long way off, and Menon said he gives the technology less than a 50% of being commercially deployed into existing U.S. fabs by the end of the decade.

“If successful, we would expect a net negative impact on ASML’s growth rates into the next decade, even accounting for the potentially higher leading-edge volumes from cheaper chip manufacturing,” Menon added.

data.title: Oil posts second straight positive week on Russian supply worries, likely Fed rate cut
data.description:
Crude oil futures posted solid gains for the week, helped by diminishing prospects of a Russia-Ukraine peace deal and growing tensions between the U.S. and Venezuela.

A Russia-Ukraine agreement looks distant, and Ukraine drones attacked Russia's Syzran refinery and the key Azov Sea port of Temryuk, which handles liquefied petroleum gas, oil products and petrochemicals, as well as grain and other bulk food commodities.

Russia's President Putin, on his first trip to India since the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, on Friday offered uninterrupted fuel supplies in an effort to maintain Indian purchases.

"Putin doesn't appear poised to relinquish any strong territorial demands, especially with Trump appearing to lean in his favor," Ritterbusch analysts said in a note. "Consequently, drone attacks on Russian oil infrastructure such as refineries, storage facilities, etc. are apt to resume," providing support for oil in the short-term.

Further out, however, Ritterbusch thinks oil production elsewhere ultimately will pull crude prices back to $55-$59/bbl - "We will repeat that fundamentals always eventually win out with time," the firm wrote.

Markets eyed a potential U.S. military incursion into Venezuela after President Trump reiterated the U.S. could take further actions to stop Venezuelan drug traffickers.

Also lifting crude is the lead-up to the Federal Reserve's next meeting, where policymakers are expected to reduce the interest rate, providing a potential boost to demand for oil products.

Friday's delayed September report of the Personal Consumption Expenditure index came in lower than expected, reinforcing expectations that Fed will lower interest rates when it meets next week.

The major oil benchmarks rose for the second straight week, as front-month Nymex January crude (CL1:COM) closed +2.6% to $60.08/bbl and front-month Brent February crude (CO1:COM) finished +2.2% to $63.75/bbl, on Friday, Nymex and Brent rose 0.7% and 0.8%, respectively.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) surged 9% for the week, as cold temperatures moved through the U.S., increasing the demand as more consumers run their heaters; front-month Nymex natural gas for January delivery +9% on the week and up 4.4% Friday to $5.289/MMBtu.

Also underpinning strength in natgas is demand for U.S. exports, Capital Economics said in a note, as "the increase in U.S. natural gas prices has come alongside a surge in LNG exports, with record high volumes of feedgas flowing into export terminals," adding U.S. LNG exports have jumped to ~15 billion cm in 2025.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

Top 15 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: T1 Energy (TE) +41.5%, Smart Sand (SND) +31.8%, Sable Offshore (SOC) +29%, USA Rare Earth (USAR) +28%, FuelCell Energy (FCEL) +24.7%, Energy Vault (NRGV) +23.8%, Critical Metals (CRML) +22.7%, Borr Drilling (BORR) +22.3%, Fluence Energy (FLNC) +22%, ProFrac Holding (ACDC) +19.7%, Fusion Fuel Green (HTOO) +19.6%, Nabors Industries (NBR) +17.1%, Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) +15.6%, Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) +15.4%, Oklo (OKLO) +14.5%.

Top 5 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: ESS Tech (GWH) -21.4%, Solaredge Technologies (SEDG) -19.2%, Sigma Lithium (SGML) -16.6%, New Era Energy & Digital (NUAI) -15.1%, Highpeak Energy (HPK) -14%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: Chevron commits to Nigeria 2025 licensing round, plans rig deployment in 2026
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) said it plans to participate in Nigeria’s next oil licensing round and deploy a drilling rig in 2026 as it seeks to expand operations in the country, Reuters reported Friday.

"Our intention is to continue to grow in Nigeria," Chevron's (CVX) Nigeria/Mid-Africa top executive Jim Swartz told Reuters after meeting the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission, citing improved regulatory clarity and security.

Chevron (CVX) announced an agreement this week to acquire a 40% stake in two offshore exploration licences from TotalEnergies (TTE) and is seeking regulatory approval to accelerate development; TotalEnergies also has expressed interest in joining the Nigeria auction.

Swartz said Chevron (CVX) plans to deploy a rig in Nigeria late next year to drill a newly discovered resource near Agbami and extend leases on existing assets.

The executive also said Chevron (CVX) did not record any oil theft or sabotage in the past year, its longest-ever period without disruptions in its Nigerian operations.

data.title: Carvana to join S&P 500, stock jumps 10% after hours
data.description:
S&P Dow Jones Indices on Friday said Carvana (CVNA) will join the benchmark S&P 500 list of companies, sending the used car retailer's stock up about 10% in aftermarket trading.

Class A shares of CVNA were last +9.9% after hours. 

Building materials provider CRH (CRH) and HVAC contractor Comfort Systems USA (FIX) will also be added to the S&P 500. 

CRH stock was +7% after hours, while shares of FIX were +1.9%. 

The companies will join the index (SP500) prior to the start of trading on December 22.

S&P Dow Jones Indices also said auto parts provider LKQ (LKQ), specialty materials firm Solstice Advanced Materials (SOLS), and flooring manufacturer Mohawk Industries (MHK) will be removed from the S&P 500.

data.title: Silver surges to new record as strong ETF inflows add to momentum
data.description:
Silver futures hit another record high Friday and logged a fourth weekly gain out of five, as strong inflows to ETFs sustain the metal's sizzling rally; gold finished roughly flat for the day and the week.

Total additions to silver-backed ETFs in the four days through Thursday already reached the highest for any full week since July, Bloomberg reported, a strong indicator of investor demand.

Silver has roughly doubled this year, with the rally accelerating in the last two months due in part to a historic squeeze in London, which has eased in recent weeks as more metal was shipped to the world's biggest silver trading hub, but other markets such as China are now seeing supply constraints.

Silver is "following the pathway of gold and many investors still believe that silver is quite cheap in relative terms," TD Securities global head of commodity strategy Bart Melek wrote, pointing to structural deficits and rising demand for electrification as supportive factors.

The metal could climb to $62/oz in the next three months "on the back of Fed cuts, robust investment demand, and physical deficit," Citi analysts said in a note.

Like gold, silver's recent surge also has been supported by rising expectations that the U.S. Federal Reserve will reduce interest rates at its meeting next week.

On Friday, the core Personal Consumption Expenditures price index - the Federal Reserve's preferred metric for measuring inflation - stayed under 3% in September, showing a moderate month-to-month rise in prices that is unlikely to prevent policymakers from considering an interest rate cut at the Fed's December 9-10 meeting.

The CME's FedWatch tool indicates an 87.2% probability of a 25-basis-point rate cut at next week's meeting.

This week, front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery ended -0.1% to $4,212.90/oz, down two of the last three weeks, while front-month Comex December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +3.5% to a new record-high settlement of $58.422/oz; on Friday, gold was flat, and silver gained 2.7%.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Kymera rises on upcoming data release for eczema asset KT-621
data.description:
• Kymera Therapeutics (KYMR) is up ~9% in after-hours trading Friday after announcing it will release phase 1b data on its atopic dermatitis (eczema) candidate KT-621 on Dec. 8.
  • A press release about the results will be issued at 7a ET that day, followed by a webcast at 8a. 
  • KT-621 is known as an oral STAT6 degrader. STAT6, according to Kymera, is "an essential transcription factor in the IL-4/IL-13 signaling pathways and the central driver of Th2 inflammation in allergic diseases"
  • Other potential indications for the asset include asthma and COPD.
